title: AI Agent API Surface
riido_task: RIID-4720 [Contracts] AI Agent policy vocabulary and client API projection
summary: AI Agent client API surface is generated from the DSL/IR/OpenAPI fixture set; manual route lists are not an authority.
codegen_rules: Control-plane API enum values and sum-type variants are contract values, not free text.
Domain DSL is the authoring surface, API IR preserves it, and OpenAPI is the generated client projection.
Generated client code is not owned by this repository.
invariants: DSL and IR operation tuples must match exactly.
OpenAPI operation tuples must match the IR projection exactly.
Every v1 operation must have an equivalent workspace-scoped v2 operation.
v2 may add workspace-specific task assignment/read-model operations beyond v1 compatibility.

loop.observation: The AI Agent policy manual route list was narrower than the current DSL/IR/OpenAPI fixture surface.
loop.hypothesis: A fixture-backed verifier can make API surface drift observable and keep prose from becoming a second source of truth.
loop.execute: Compare DSL, IR, and OpenAPI operation tuples, count v1/v2 surfaces, verify v2-only operations, and verify ClientStreamEvent variants.
loop.evaluate: CI fails when route count, route tuple parity, stream variants, generated reader, or workflow artifact binding drift.
loop.retrospective: Future AI Agent endpoints should update the DSL/IR/OpenAPI fixture and this manifest before reader text changes.
